articles.body: Item 5 of 5 Barricades are placed near the U.S. Capitol at sunrise as day breaks ahead of Fourth of July celebrations, marking the 250th anniversary of U.S. Independence, in Washington, D.C., U.S., July 4, 2026. REUTERS/Nathan Howard [5/5] Barricades are placed near the U.S. Capitol at sunrise as day breaks ahead of Fourth of July celebrations, marking the 250th anniversary of U.S. Independence, in Washington, D.C., U.S., July 4,... Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Read more Summary Trump promises 'most spectacular TRUMP RALLY of them all' Patriot Front group announces its arrival in Washington Philadelphia offers free cupcakes, New York has tall ships WASHINGTON, July 4 (Reuters) - U.S. President Donald Trump marks the country's ​250th anniversary on Saturday with a political rally on a fenced-off National Mall in Washington, capping a weeks-long ‌celebration that has been widely criticized as divisive. Across the U.S., Americans plan to celebrate the patriotic holiday with fireworks and parades. Philadelphia, where the Declaration of Independence from Britain was signed on July 4, 1776, will offer free cupcakes and a six-hour pop-music concert, while New York will ​feature tall ships from around the world. The Reuters Daily Briefing newsletter provides all the news you need to start your day. Sign up here. In the nation's capital, Trump will place himself at the center of the ​celebration. The president has billed his evening appearance among the city's iconic monuments as "the most spectacular TRUMP ⁠RALLY of them all," accompanied by military flyovers and a super-sized fireworks display. Washington's annual July 4 celebration typically draws hundreds ​of thousands of people. Visitors this year must contend with stepped-up security, possible thunderstorms and temperatures that could top 100F (38C). Groups coming to Washington on Saturday included ​the white nationalist organization Patriot Front. The group posted on social media that it had arrived in the capital, and Reuters photographers saw hundreds of people dressed in Patriot Front outfits traveling on D.C. metro trains. The organization, founded in 2017, is known for its aesthetics and heavily organized ​flash-mob style demonstrations. Past U.S. presidents have steered clear of the celebration, but Trump has blurred the line between official commemoration ​and campaign-style politics. A nonpartisan body set up in 2016 to handle the 250th anniversary has been largely sidelined by the Trump administration's Freedom ‌250 ⁠group, which has fenced off much of the 1.5-mile National Mall for a " Great American State Fair " featuring attractions such as a Ferris wheel alongside displays by conservative groups and defense contractors. Several Democratic-led states declined to send delegations, and many performers who had been scheduled to appear dropped out , citing concerns about partisanship. Thousands were on hand for Trump's kickoff rally on June 24 but since then crowd sizes at ​the event have sometimes been ​modest. Other activities with Freedom 250 ⁠branding include a faith rally featuring mostly conservative Christian speakers, and multiple sports events , including a card of mixed martial arts bouts on the White House grounds for Trump's 80th birthday. An IndyCar race in Washington is ​scheduled for August. The Freedom 250 organization also sponsored "Freedom Trucks" that critics say paint an overly religious ​version of American ⁠history and gloss over issues such as slavery and racial injustice. A Reuters/Ipsos poll found that a majority of Americans, including three-quarters of Democrats and half of Republicans, think the events celebrating the country's 250th anniversary have grown too political. Trump has sought to remake ⁠wide swaths ​of the capital city ahead of the 250th celebration, with mixed results. ​Many fountains and statues have been renovated, but problems have beset a much-touted $15 million renovation of the Lincoln Memorial's Reflecting Pool and security cameras and soldiers now ​stand watch over its peeling paint and algae-fouled waters. Reporting by Andy Sullivan; Editing by Sergio Non, Alistair Bell and David Gregorio Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: United States X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights Andy Sullivan Thomson Reuters Andy covers politics and policy in Washington. His work has been cited in Supreme Court briefs, political attack ads and at least one Saturday Night Live skit. Email X

articles.body: DUBAI, July 4 (Reuters) - Mourners thronged a vast prayer complex in Tehran on Saturday as the week-long ​funeral ceremonies of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began with the national anthem, religious ‌eulogies and readings from the Koran. Iran is staging mass funeral processions for Khamenei – whose 37-year reign was brought to an end in February by the first airstrike of the war launched by the U.S. and Israel – in a show ​of public devotion to the Islamic Republic's theocratic state and revolutionary zeal. The Reuters Inside Track newsletter is your essential guide during the World Cup. Sign up here. Television footage showed ​his coffin draped with the Iranian flag and topped with his black turban. ⁠It was placed, along with four other coffins of his slain family members, on a large ​black platform that resembled the Kaaba, the cube-shaped structure at the centre of Islam's holiest site ​in Mecca. The vast courtyard of the complex, the Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla, was filled with mourners, many waving Iranian flags and carrying photographs of the slain leader. "'Death to America' chants echoed through Tehran's Mosalla on the day ​of the farewell to 'Mr. Martyr'," state broadcaster Seda va Sima said. Item 10 of 10 People attend a public farewell ceremony to pay their respects to late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed on February 28 in Israeli and U.S. airstrikes, at the Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla, in Tehran, Iran July 4, 2026. REUTERS/Murad Sezer Foreign media in Iran operate under guidelines set by the Ministry of Culture and Islamic Guidance, which regulates press activity and permissions [10/10] People attend a public farewell ceremony to pay their respects to late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed on February 28 in Israeli and U.S. airstrikes, at the Imam Khomeini... Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Read more In video posts on other state ​media news sites, mourners were also heard chanting: "Our slogan is one word: Revenge, revenge," and "We will kill, we will ‌kill ⁠he who killed our Imam." Water misted from rooftops to cool mourners in the summer heat. Khamenei`s coffin will remain in the Mosalla until Sunday evening. His body was expected to be taken to Qom, Najaf and Kerbala, the great Shi'ite centres of Iran and Iraq, before being laid to rest ​on Thursday in Mashhad, ​home to the country's ⁠holiest pilgrim shrine. The coffin was unveiled late on Thursday to a throng of sobbing supporters, who were swaying and beating their heads in time ​to a sung lament as flowers were thrown from the bier into ​the crowd. On ⁠Friday the coffin was laid in state in the great prayer hall built to honour his predecessor, Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini. Authorities plan to mobilise millions of people for big processions over the coming days, offering ⁠transport, ​food and lodging to buoy the numbers. The new supreme leader, ​Khamenei's son Mojtaba Khamenei , has not been seen in any new image since being wounded in the strike that killed his ​father. Reporting by Parisa Hafezi, Ahmed Elimam and Eman Abouhassira; Writing by Raju Gopalakrishnan; Editing by Tom Hogue Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Media & Telecom X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: Item 4 of 4 Russian President Vladimir Putin visits a command post of Russia's joint force grouping, in the course of the Russia-Ukraine conflict, at an unidentified location, in this still image taken from a video released July 3, 2026. Kremlin.ru/Handout via REUTERS [4/4] Russian President Vladimir Putin visits a command post of Russia's joint force grouping, in the course of the Russia-Ukraine conflict, at an unidentified location, in this still image taken from a... Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Read more Summary Russian military says it captured key city of Kostiantynivka Says progress made in securing Lyman Russia's Putin says Kostiantynivka of strategic importance Putin says Ukraine strikes mean more security zones needed July 3 (Reuters) - Russia's military told Russian President Vladimir Putin on ​Friday that its forces had taken control of Kostiantynivka in eastern Ukraine, a key locality whose capture Moscow has ‌long sought in its advance through the Donetsk region. Putin , shown in videos visiting a command post and receiving a report from top commanders, praised the city's capture as an important strategic achievement. The Reuters Iran Briefing newsletter keeps you informed with the latest developments and analysis of the Iran war. Sign up here. He also said Russian forces had to increase the scale of security zones in response to intensified Ukrainian long-range strikes, mainly ​against Russia's oil industry. Ukraine made no comment on Russia's claim to have seized Kostiantynivka. General Valery Gerasimov, chief of Russia's General ​Staff, announced the city's capture in a report on the conduct of the war, now in its ⁠fifth year. Gerasimov said the southern group of forces was carrying out offensive operations to "liberate" all of the Donetsk region, part of the ​broader Donbas region over which Russia has vowed to secure control as a key aim of its war effort. "The troops of the group have ​liberated the city of Kostiantynivka, one of the main defensive hubs of the enemy within the Sloviansk-Kramatorsk-Kostiantynivka fortified area", Gerasimov said. Russia's military has for some time reported that its troops were in control of parts of Kostiantynivka, one of several cities seen as a network of key fortifications in Ukraine's defence of ​the region. Putin described the city as having an important strategic significance. "We all know that this city is a key transport and large ​industrial centre of Donbas," Putin said. RUSSIAN PICTURES SHOW SOLDIERS IN CITY The Defence Ministry posted images on Telegram that it said were scenes from Kostiantynivka, including ‌pictures of ⁠Russian soldiers holding national flags by shattered buildings. Gerasimov also told Putin that Russian forces were closing in on Lyman, a town about 70 km (45 miles) to the north which he said was "of key logistical and strategic importance for further advances in this direction". The head of Russia's northern troops, Yevgeny Nikiforov, said his forces had "not yet fully managed to resolve the task" of preventing Ukrainian drone strikes that have hit ​oil installations and caused fuel ​shortages in Russia. Putin responded by ⁠saying: "The more attacks the enemy tries to carry out on our civilian facilities ... the larger a security zone we`ll have to establish in the neighbouring territory." Nikiforov told Putin that Russian troops were making progress ​in securing border areas in the Kharkiv and Sumy regions, where Moscow has called for expanded ​buffer zones. Ukrainian President Volodymyr ⁠Zelenskiy and other officials have said Russia's advances in eastern Ukraine have slowed considerably since the beginning of the year and Kyiv's forces have even recaptured some territory. Putin, in his remarks, dismissed Kyiv's statements as an "information campaign in which it puts on display supposed successes". Zelenskiy sent an ⁠open letter ​to Putin last month seeking a direct meeting but the Kremlin leader rejected the ​proposal. U.S.-brokered attempts to move towards a peace deal have been put on hold during the conflict in Iran, but both Moscow and Kyiv say they are anticipating a ​visit soon by the two top negotiators, Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. Reporting by Reuters; Editing by Edmund Klamann, Bill Berkrot and Tom Hogue Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Aerospace & Defense X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: Item 1 of 3 People gather on a beach, as a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz is visible, near the beach of Bandar Abbas, Iran, May 31, 2026. Amirhosein Khorgooi/ISNA/WANA (West Asia News Agency) via REUTERS [1/3] People gather on a beach, as a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz is visible, near the beach of Bandar Abbas, Iran, May 31, 2026. Amirhosein Khorgooi/ISNA/WANA (West Asia News Agency) via REUTERS Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Summary U.S. strikes targeted Iranian radar sites after drone attacks on maritime traffic, Central Command said Trump says Tehran has less than 22% of its missiles left Iran demands Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon as condition for regional ceasefire, supports Hezbollah DUBAI/WASHINGTON, June 6 (Reuters) - U.S. forces struck Iranian coastal ​radar sites on Saturday after shooting down drones launched by Iran toward the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. military said, in the latest ‌escalation complicating efforts to end the war between the two countries. The U.S. military believes the four Iranian drones were targeting regional maritime traffic, a U.S. official told Reuters. U.S. Central Command said on X that the U.S. then struck Iran's surveillance sites in Goruk and Qeshm Island, which are both on the Strait of Hormuz. Sign up here. The U.S. and Iran have been engaged in largely indirect negotiations ​to secure an interim deal to halt the three-month-old war that would leave issues including Iran's nuclear programme to further negotiations. As part of any agreement, ​Tehran wants access to billions of dollars in oil revenue, waivers on sanctions on crude exports, the lifting of a ⁠U.S. blockade on its ports and leverage over the strait. Iran has effectively blocked the strait, where about a fifth of the world's oil transited before ​the war. U.S. President Donald Trump is facing mounting domestic political pressure due to rising gas prices to bring the unpopular war to an end. He told NBC that while most of Iran's ​drone and missile manufacturing facilities had been destroyed, the Iranians still have access to about a fifth of their missiles. "They have some missiles, they have some drones. I would say percentage wise, maybe 21%-22% of their missiles. It's a lot of missiles, but it's not what it was when we first attacked," Trump told NBC News' "Meet the Press" program, according ​to excerpts released by the network on Friday. When asked why Iran`s leaders — if as desperate as he has portrayedthem — were not more inclined to strike a ​deal, Trump said: "Because they are strong. They're proud. There are things they never thought they'd be doing that they're going to have to do, they've got no choice, and ‌it takes ⁠a little while." Israel and the U.S. launched the war with strikes on Iran in late February. FIGHTING FLARES ACROSS REGION DESPITE CEASEFIRES In a parallel conflict in Lebanon, Iran-aligned armed group Hezbollah said on Friday it had carried out two attacks on Israeli troops in south Lebanon, including near the recently captured Beaufort Castle , while Lebanese security services said Israeli airstrikes hit towns across southern Lebanon. Iran has reaffirmed support for Hezbollah while demanding that Israel withdraw from southern Lebanon. Tehran has made a ceasefire between ​Israel and Hezbollah a condition for any peace ​deal with Washington to resolve ⁠the regional war, now in its fourth month, and restart shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The latest round of fighting between Hezbollah and Israel erupted at the start of March, two days after the U.S. and Israel launched strikes against ​Iran. Hezbollah said its actions were in support of Tehran. Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem this week rejected a U.S.-brokered pact between ​Israel and the Lebanese ⁠government to halt the fighting in Lebanon. The deal did not provide for an Israeli withdrawal and Hezbollah had not been party to the negotiations. Israel has kept up strikes in southern Lebanon, and it has said its forces would not withdraw or halt operations in the country amid increasing friction with the U.S. Lebanon's parliament speaker and Hezbollah ⁠ally Nabih Berri ​said on Friday he would agree to the withdrawal of the group from southern Lebanon ​if Israeli troops simultaneously left territory they occupy in the country. Along with Lebanon, residents of Gaza, northern Israel and Kuwait have all been under fire this week, despite U.S.-arranged ceasefires that Trump said ​involved "shooting in a more moderate manner", rather than a total halt to fighting. Reporting by Reuters bureaus; Writing by Aidan Lewis and Nathan Layne; Editing by Cynthia Osterman Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Middle East X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights Phil Stewart Thomson Reuters Phil Stewart has reported from more than 60 countries, including Afghanistan, Ukraine, Syria, Iraq, Pakistan, Russia, Saudi Arabia, China and South Sudan. articles.body: Item 1 of 4 A view from the International Space Station October 9, 2024. Matthew Dominick/NASA/Handout via REUTERS [1/4] A view from the International Space Station October 9, 2024. Matthew Dominick/NASA/Handout via REUTERS Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Summary Companies NASA reversed evacuation after assessing air leak risk with Roscosmos Roscosmos detected two leaks, sealed one, said no immediate threat to crew Disagreement over repair methods led to safe-haven order Astronauts have never evacuated ISS in its 27-year history Air leak rate doubled Friday, heightening concern among mission controllers WASHINGTON, June 5 (Reuters) - A worsening air leak aboard the International Space Station ‌prompted five astronauts to take shelter and prepare for evacuation for roughly two hours on Friday as Russia attempted to fix a crack on its portion of the orbital laboratory, NASA said. The four astronauts of NASA`s Crew-12 mission aboard the station — two Americans, a French astronaut and a Russian cosmonaut — along with another U.S. astronaut were ordered by NASA mission ​control at 9:04 a.m. ET (1304 GMT) on Friday to enter their SpaceX-built Crew Dragon spacecraft docked to the station, NASA spokesperson Bethany Stevens said. Sign up here. NASA ​reversed that order roughly two hours later and told the astronauts they could return to the station as the agency ⁠and its Russian counterparts examined the rate of leaking air. NASA and Russia's space agency Roscosmos, the station's two primary operators, have debated for months over ​the cause and potential fixes of small air leaks aboard Russia's Zvezda service module, a key structure of the ISS, a football field-size orbital laboratory ​where astronauts live and work in space. Roscosmos said on Friday that its experts had detected two leaks aboard the ISS but that there was no immediate threat to the crew. The first leak was quickly sealed, and preparations were underway to seal the second one, Roscosmos said, adding that there was no threat to the spacecraft's systems. The air leaks ​have been relatively minor in recent months but escalated on Friday from a pound of air per day to two pounds, according to a senior ​NASA official who asked not to be named. The ISS is currently home to seven astronauts from two missions, including the Crew‑12 team — NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Jack ‌Hathaway, European ⁠Space Agency astronaut Sophie Adenot, and Roscosmos cosmonaut Andrey Fedyaev — who arrived in February. The other crew of one U.S. astronaut, Christopher Williams, and two cosmonauts, Sergey Kud-Sverchkov and Sergei Mikayev, arrived in November. Kud-Sverchkov and Mikayev, who did not execute evacuation procedures, were planning to use a saw to reach an area where they believe they can access the crack leaking air, the NASA official said. NASA officials disagreed with this method, the NASA official said, prompting mission control ​in Houston to order safe-haven procedures. Stevens ​said NASA reversed the safe-haven order ⁠and told astronauts they could return the space station once Roscosmos paused its efforts to repair the crack. "We look forward to working with Roscosmos on a collaborative approach to address the leaks," she said. Safe-haven orders are rare on ​the International Space Station, though pieces of space debris that risk colliding with the ISS and smaller changes ​in air leak rates ⁠have triggered the process in recent years. Astronauts have never had to evacuate the ISS in its 27 year history. Legislation is before the U.S. Congress that would extend the planned life of the space station for two years, until 2032, to give companies more time to develop a replacement. The bill has the backing of ⁠Senator Ted ​Cruz, a Republican, and Senator Maria Cantwell, a Democrat - the chair and ranking member, respectively, ​of the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science and Transportation. The legislation is part of the committee's focus on rivaling China's growing footprint in space. Leaders in the U.S. Senate and House of Representatives ​are working to reach consensus on the proposed legislation. Reporting by Joey Roulette; Additional reporting by Karen Freifeld; Editing by Chizu Nomiyama, Joe Brock and Nick Zieminski Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Science X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights Joey Roulette Thomson Reuters Joey Roulette is a space reporter for Reuters covering the business and politics of the global space industry, often focusing on space power competition and how commercial interests intersect with international relations. He was part of a team that won the 2024 Pulitzer Prize in national reporting for Reuters' coverage of Elon Musk's business empire. On the space beat for roughly a decade, Joey previously worked for the New York Times, the Verge, and various publications in Florida. Email

articles.body: A woman holds a Hezbollah flag during a rally in support of Iran's Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, in Tehran, Iran, June 4, 2026. Majid Asgaripour/WANA (West Asia News Agency) via REUTERS Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Summary Conflict in Lebanon has run in parallel to the Iran war Israel has kept up strikes in southern Lebanon after invading U.S. forces board tanker, Iran fires warning shots U.N. warns that standoff is pushing millions closer to hunger DUBAI, June 5 (Reuters) - Iran has reaffirmed support for its Lebanese ​ally Hezbollah and demanded Israel withdraw from southern Lebanon, underscoring complications facing an interim deal to end the broader conflict between the U.S. and Iran. Iran has made a ‌ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah a condition for any peace deal with Washington to resolve the regional war, now in its fourth month, and restart shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Sign up here. The latest round of fighting between Hezbollah and Israel erupted at the start of March, two days after the U.S. and Israel launched strikes against Iran. Hezbollah said its actions were in support of Tehran. "This war will end only when it ends in Lebanon as well," Iranian ​Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told Lebanese TV station Al Mayadeen late on Thursday. "The end of the war on Lebanon must be accompanied by the withdrawal of Israeli forces from ​the territories they have occupied," he said. The comments came after Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem rejected a U.S.-brokered pact between Israel and the Lebanese government to ⁠halt the fighting in Lebanon. The deal did not provide for an Israeli withdrawal and Hezbollah had not been party to the negotiations. Israel has kept up strikes in southern Lebanon, and has said ​its forces would not withdraw or halt operations in the country amid increasing friction with the U.S. Hezbollah said on Friday it had carried out two attacks on Israeli troops in south Lebanon, including near the ​recently captured Beaufort Castle , while Lebanese security services said Israeli airstrikes hit towns across southern Lebanon. FIGHTING FLARES ACROSS REGION DESPITE CEASEFIRES Mohsen Rezaei, an adviser to Iran's supreme leader, said Hezbollah had "made great sacrifices in the recent war and it is our ally. Therefore, we support Hezbollah and remain firmly committed to our obligations toward it." In comments reported by the semi-official Mehr news agency, he cautioned Israel against following through on threats to resume strikes against the ​Lebanese capital, Beirut. "Today we again warn this sinister regime to leave Lebanon. They should know that Lebanon will be an inseparable part of any agreement and any ceasefire." Lebanon's parliament speaker and Hezbollah ​ally Nabih Berri said on Friday he would agree to the withdrawal of the Iran-backed group from southern Lebanon if Israeli troops simultaneously left territory they occupy in the country. In Washington, President Donald Trump told reporters he ‌believed progress was ⁠being made in Lebanon and the country deserved to have peace. Along with Lebanon, residents of Gaza, northern Israel and Kuwait have all been under fire this week, despite U.S.-arranged ceasefires that Trump said involved "shooting in a more moderate manner," rather than a total halt to fighting. On Friday, Iran's navy said it had fired warning shots at U.S. destroyers in the Gulf of Oman to counter "maritime mischief and harassment and the hijacking of commercial vessels and oil tankers". Earlier, U.S. forces said they had boarded an oil tanker in the Indian Ocean and said they would continue to block "vessels providing material support to ​Iran". In Oman, an alleged drone attack forced the suspension of ​oil loading at the Mina al Fahal ⁠terminal after an explosion, sources said, before normal operations resumed. INTERIM DEAL WOULD LEAVE COMPLEX ISSUES FOR LATER After the U.S. and Israel launched the war against Iran on February 28, Iran fired missiles and drones against Gulf states hosting U.S. bases and largely stopped shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Trade ​remains at a fraction of its former levels through the waterway, which previously carried about a fifth of global oil and liquefied natural gas ​supplies. The conflict has driven ⁠up oil prices and disrupted supply chains for other products. The U.N. World Food Programme warned on Friday that it was pushing millions of people closer to hunger due to rising fuel and transport costs. The U.S. and Iran have been engaged in largely indirect negotiations to secure an interim deal to halt the war that would leave issues including Iran's nuclear programme to further negotiations. As part of any agreement, Tehran wants access to ⁠billions of dollars ​in oil revenue, waivers on sanctions on crude exports, a lifting of a U.S. blockade on its ports and ​leverage over the strait. Trump, who faces domestic pressure over an unpopular war , has said his top priority is to stop Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons. Iran says its atomic program is for peaceful purposes. Iranian parliament deputy speaker Hamid-Reza Haji Babaei said on Friday that ​uranium enrichment was Iran's right, and that Trump had failed to understand that Iran's "most powerful atomic bomb" was the Strait of Hormuz. Reporting by Reuters bureaus; Writing by Aidan Lewis; Editing by Sharon Singleton Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Middle East X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: Item 1 of 6 Law enforcement officers stand amid smoke from a smoke grenade released to disperse demonstrators in front of the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) headquarters in Portland, Oregon, U.S., October 4, 2025. REUTERS/Carlos Barria TPX IMAGES OF THE DAY Pulitzer Prize Finalist for Breaking News Photography [1/6] Law enforcement officers stand amid smoke from a smoke grenade released to disperse demonstrators in front of the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) headquarters in Portland, Oregon,... Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Read more Summary Companies Senate Republicans pass $70 billion in extra immigration enforcement funding Democrats had pushed to eliminate Trump's $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund Some Senate Republicans sought their own amendments to end the fund House could debate bill next week WASHINGTON, June 5 (Reuters) - The U.S. Senate handed President Donald Trump a victory early Friday morning, passing a bill that would provide the Department of Homeland Security ​with an additional $70 billion for immigration enforcement and sending it to the House of Representatives for final consideration. The Senate voted 52-47 to approve the legislation, with no support from ‌Democrats and no provision to ban a $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund that could compensate Trump's political allies for allegations that the government mistreated them. One Republican voted against the bill. Sign up here. Senate Republican Leader John Thune said the fund was a "settled issue," citing acting Attorney General Todd Blanche's congressional testimony this week that the Department of Justice would not move forward with it, though Democrats have said his word was insufficient. Trump subsequently said he intended to nominate Blanche to lead the Justice Department - an action that would require Senate confirmation. ​Thune has indicated that it could be an uphill battle to get such a nomination approved in the Senate. "I find it very hard to believe that they're going to submit somebody ​who sat in front of a committee in the House and made definitive statements about this and then somehow all of a sudden turn around and ⁠go back on them," Thune told reporters. "I don't think that's going to happen." Nonetheless, Trump himself on Wednesday said he the establishment of the fund was important. EXTRA MONEY FOR DEPORTATION CRACKDOWN Republicans have accused Democrats of "defunding" Immigration ​and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol, despite the agencies having a combined $100 billion in unspent funds that were part of a larger DHS spending package enacted last year by Republicans, who control Congress. The House is not ​expected to take up the measure before next week. Much of Thursday's long debate over the ICE funding bill was overshadowed by efforts from Democrats, and some Republicans, to insert language unrelated to immigration. Those proposals revolved around prohibiting the use of federal funds and even private donations for building the lavish, 90,000 square-foot ballroom on White House grounds that Trump wants. Senators also debated provisions making it illegal for federal dollars to be used for the "anti-weaponization" fund. None of those amendments were ​approved. The funding provided by the bill would help pay for Trump's controversial migrant deportation crackdown over the next three years. Lawmakers began voting on amendments to the immigration bill in a "vote-a-rama" session early on Thursday that ​culminated in the vote on the underlying measure in the early hours of Friday. An initial move by Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer to kill the "anti-weaponization" fund, which Democrats call a "slush fund" for Trump's allies, brought the session to ‌a largely procedural ⁠halt for hours after Republican Senator Susan Collins voted for the motion. She was later joined by fellow Republicans Jon Husted and Dan Sullivan. Schumer's measure failed in a 50-49 vote but exposed the political turmoil among rank-and-file Senate Republicans. Some of them sought their own amendments to eliminate the fund permanently, five months before the November midterm elections. Collins, Husted and Sullivan all face competitive races for reelection at a time when Trump's approval rating is down, even among Republicans. "Republicans refused to permanently outlaw Trump`s $2 billion slush fund, leaving taxpayers to rely on nothing more than a promise from Donald Trump`s personal fixer," Schumer said in a statement after the ​final vote, referring to Blanche. The fund has already been put ​on hold by the White House and ⁠Justice Department. But on Wednesday, Trump declined to say whether it had actually been terminated, telling reporters: "I love it. I think it's so important." Republican Senator Thom Tillis, who opposed Schumer's motion, told reporters he would not support passage of the funding bill without a Republican amendment vote to codify Blanche's congressional testimony . Tillis argued that ​failing to do so would place a burden on congressional Republicans up for re-election in November who are worried about a voter backlash to the ​fund. OPPONENTS CALL TRUMP FUND 'IMMEDIATE AND DIRE ⁠THREAT' Nearly all of the immigration bill's funding would go to DHS's ICE and Border Patrol agencies that are carrying out the Trump administration's vigorous deportations throughout the United States. Tillis later offered his own amendment to reallocate the controversial Trump fund's resources to fraud-enforcement operations. It failed in an 84-15 vote, while garnering support from 12 Republicans. Republican Senator Bill Cassidy, who proposed his own amendment to end the fund, joined Democratic Senator Cory Booker in a ⁠friend-of-the-court brief urging ​U.S. District Judge Leonie Brinkema to maintain the block on Trump's fund that she imposed last week. They argued the fund "presents an ​immediate and dire threat to our constitutional order and the authority of Congress". A number of recent actions by Trump have prompted open criticism from some Republicans, from seeking $1 billion in taxpayer funding for a White House ballroom and security upgrades to his decision to ​nominate Blanche as attorney general and name political ally Bill Pulte as U.S. intelligence chief. articles.body: Item 1 of 2 People walk past an anti-U.S. mural on a street in Tehran, Iran, June 1, 2026. Majid Asgaripour/WANA (West Asia News Agency) via REUTERS [1/2] People walk past an anti-U.S. mural on a street in Tehran, Iran, June 1, 2026. Majid Asgaripour/WANA (West Asia News Agency) via REUTERS Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Summary Companies Iran taking 'stern' approach in negotiations, report says Trump says talks for a deal are continuing Iran pushing for a limited interim agreement, sources say Israel continues strikes on southern Lebanon DUBAI, June 2 (Reuters) - Iran is reviewing a proposed agreement with the U.S. to halt their war, Iranian media ​reported on Tuesday, after U.S. President Donald Trump said talks to reach a deal were continuing. More than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched ‌strikes against Iran, the conflict has hardened into a stalemate while efforts to negotiate an interim deal have proved inconclusive, leaving the Strait of Hormuz largely shut. Sign up here. Iran has not yet responded to a proposed final text of the temporary deal, and was taking a "stern" approach given what it sees as a history of U.S. non-compliance and longstanding mistrust, Mehr News Agency cited a source as saying. Trump said on Monday that negotiations with Iran were ​continuing and there would be a deal over the next week to extend a ceasefire agreed in early April and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Since mid-March, Trump has ​repeatedly said he is close to signing a peace agreement, though any such deal would postpone thorny issues including the future of ⁠Iran's nuclear programme . A ceasefire has largely held since early April, but Iran and the U.S. have exchanged strikes several times over the past week. Oil prices fell more than 1% on Tuesday, ​paring the previous day's sharp gains. A senior International Energy Agency official warned that global oil inventories could hit historically low levels. ISRAEL KEEPS UP STRIKES IN LEBANON The war that began on February ​28 has killed thousands of people, mainly in Iran and Lebanon. It has caused global economic pain by pushing up energy prices since Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, which previously carried about a fifth of global supplies of oil and liquefied natural gas. It also triggered the latest round of conflict between Israel and Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, with Israel pursuing its deepest incursion into Lebanon in 25 years. On Tuesday, ​Israel kept up strikes on a string of towns in southern Lebanon, Lebanese security sources said, a day after U.S. mediation appeared to have averted an immediate escalation of that war. A ​partial ceasefire announced by Lebanon on Monday would entail Israel refraining from strikes on Beirut and Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital, while the Iran-aligned group would halt its attacks on Israel. But the ‌announcement failed ⁠to reassure many Lebanese, 1.2 million of whom have been displaced, and the din of an Israeli drone over Beirut kept residents on edge on Tuesday. "Every time we return to our homes, there is a warning for us to be displaced again," said Faten Al Chehime, who fled to a displacement camp from her home in Beirut's southern suburbs on Monday, only two weeks after returning there. Lebanon said it would seek to expand the ceasefire in talks with Israel in Washington on Wednesday. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is facing criticism domestically over any agreement ​to hold back from further attacks on ​Beirut, ahead of an election later this ⁠year that he is projected to lose. IRAN PUSHES FOR LIMITED DEAL In the wider war, Iran is pushing for a limited interim agreement as it tries to ease mounting economic pressure while avoiding major concessions on its nuclear programme, according to Iranian sources. As part of any deal, Tehran ​is seeking an end to hostilities across all fronts including Lebanon, access to billions of dollars in oil revenues, waivers on ​crude exports, a lifting ⁠of a U.S. blockade on its ports, and continued leverage over the Strait of Hormuz. Trump is under pressure to reopen the strait and curb U.S. fuel prices while not making concessions to Iran. Iran's elite Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said on Tuesday that 24 vessels had transited the strait in the past 24 hours, after obtaining permission from the Guard's navy. Iran threatened on Monday to expand its blockade ⁠to the Bab ​El Mandeb Strait, another chokepoint at the mouth of the Red Sea, if Israel resumed strikes on ​Beirut. Highlighting the risk at sea, the world's largest shipping group MSC said on Tuesday that one of its vessels was struck by two projectiles while in Iraq's Umm Qasr port the previous day. Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed responsibility for the ​attack, saying it was carried out in retaliation for a U.S. attack on an Iranian vessel in the Gulf of Oman. REUTERS/Alina Smutko Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab KYIV, June 2 (Reuters) - Kyiv was under heavy Russian attack early on Tuesday, ​with a large pillar of smoke seen rising ‌from the Ukrainian capital and authorities urging residents to seek shelter, according to witnesses and officials. Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said a fire ​had broken out in the Podil district on the ​grounds of a non-residential property and a nine-storey ⁠apartment building was on fire after debris apparently struck the ​roof. Sign up here. "In the Obolon district, cars are burning after being struck ​by falling missile debris. There are also fires at two locations in open areas, including one near a kindergarten," Klitschko said on Telegram. Ukrainian ​President Volodymyr Zelenskiy on Monday reiterated warnings of a possible ​massive Russian strike and urged residents to pay special attention to air ‌raid ⁠alerts. "Intelligence warnings regarding Russian strikes remain in effect. A massive strike is possible, they have prepared one," Zelenskiy said in his nightly video address. "Our defenders are ready 24/7 to ​the fullest extent ​possible with ⁠the supplies currently available." Russia last week warned that it intended to launch "systematic strikes" on targets ​in Kyiv linked to the Ukrainian military ​as well ⁠as decision-making centres, and urged foreigners to leave. It said the action was in response to a drone strike last week ⁠on ​a dormitory in Ukraine's Russian-held Luhansk ​region which killed 21 people. Ukraine denied carrying out the attack. articles.body: Ousmane Sonko speaks after he was appointed prime minister by Senegal's newly-elected President Bassirou Diomaye Faye, in Dakar, Senegal April 2, 2024. REUTERS/Abdou Karim Ndoye/File Photo Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab DAKAR, June 1 (Reuters) - Senegal's Pastef political party will not participate in the country's new government, recently ​ousted Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko, the party president, said ‌on Monday, raising the prospect of further political turmoil amid a daunting debt crisis. Sonko said in a post on X that he ​met on Monday with President Bassirou Diomaye Faye, his ​ally-turned-rival, and that "points of disagreement" emerged about the role ⁠of Pastef, which holds a large parliamentary majority, within ​the executive branch. Sign up here. Therefore Pastef "will not participate in the next government ​and will not be represented by any ministers," Sonko said. "We wish the new team every success." Faye sacked Sonko and dissolved the government on May 22, ​then appointed seasoned economist Ahmadou Al Aminou Lo as ​a replacement. Lawmakers rebelled against Faye's decision last week by reinstating Sonko as a ‌member ⁠of parliament and overwhelmingly backing him as speaker with the support of 132 lawmakers in the 165-member assembly. The upheaval, following months of mounting tensions between Faye and Sonko, comes as ​Senegal tries to ​navigate economic ⁠woes stemming from the discovery in 2024 of misreported debt by the previous government. The International Monetary ​Fund froze its $1.8 billion lending program with ​Senegal following ⁠the discovery of the debt, which pushed the country's end-2024 debt level to 132% of its economic output. Senegal expects to ⁠resume talks ​with the IMF next week and ​hopes to reach agreement on key points by June 30, the finance minister ​said last month. articles.body: La calor tornarà a ser intensa aquest dilluns. La previsió del temps anuncia una jornada de cel ras i màximes de més de 35 graus al prelitoral. La temperatura superarà clarament els 32 o 33 graus a la costa. També et pot interessar Calor sense aturador a Barcelona: ja és aquí la segona onada de l’estiu a Catalunya Manel Cascante Torrella 4 minuts de lectura Previsió horària per a Barcelona 6 h: matinada tòrrida, més de 25 graus al centre de la ciutat 9 h: cel ras i calor forta des de bon matí 12 h: calor intensa, però poca humitat 15 h: el termòmetre tocarà sostre 18 h: més humitat prop del mar 21 h: sensació d’estiu marcada Cel clar i calor seca Cel ras i calor intensa arreu . La temperatura superarà els 35 graus a la plana vallesana i es mourà entre els 33 i 35 graus a la costa. Calor seca, poca humitat. Vent feble i bon estat de la mar. El Servei Meteorològic de Catalunya actualitza l'avís de situació meteorològica de perill ( #avisosSMP ) per calor Avisos vigents: de dilluns a dimecres Grau de perill màxim: 4/6 L'episodi es mantindrà com a mínim fins dijous i es correspondrà a una onada de calor. pic.twitter.com/uN3UWbKV7p — Meteocat (@meteocat) July 5, 2026 També et pot interessar GRÀFICS | L’estiu del 2026 versus el del 2003 a Barcelona, fins ara el més calorós Manel Cascante Torrella 2 minuts de lectura La temperatura no tocarà sostre fins dijous o divendres No s’esperen canvis significatius de cara als pròxims dies . Calor intensa arreu i predomini del sol. A partir de dimarts es formaran algunes tempestes als Pirineus, lluny del litoral i del prelitoral central. La temperatura tocarà sostre entre dijous i divendres. Petit respir a partir del cap de setmana.
articles.headline: Setmana de calor intensa: cel ras i màximes de fins a 35 graus

articles.body: Afegeix betevé com a font preferida El ciclista mexicà de l’equip UAE Isaac del Toro ha estat el guanyador de la segona etapa del  Tour de França , que ha finalitzat a Barcelona . Després de pujar tres vegades al Castell de Montjuïc, Del Toro ha arribat primer a la meta i, just enganxat a ell i en segona posició, hi ha arribat  Tadej Pogacar . El tercer ha estat  Remco Evenepoel . A la general el ciclista danès  Vingegaard  es manté líder amb sis segons d’avantatge respecte a Pogacar. La segona etapa del Tour surt de Tarragona La segona etapa del Tour de França ha sortit des de Tarragona i ràpidament tres ciclistes s’han escapat del gran grup. Han estat Felix Engelhardt  (Jayco AlUla), Frank van den Broek  (Picnic PostNL) i Alex Molenaar  (Caja Rural – Seguros RGA). De fet, han anat augmentant l’avantatge i, un cop s’ha arribat al Garraf, mantenien uns tres minuts i mig respecte al gran grup.  També et pot interessar Jonas Vingegaard i el Visma, guanyadors de la primera etapa del Tour de França disputada a Barcelona Carles Alcoyent Corominas 1 minut de lectura Arriba el coll de Begues Aquest avantatge s’ha reduït aproximadament un minut quan s’han començat a pujar les primeres rampes del  coll de Begue s i l’UAE de Tadej Pogacar ha marcat un ritme molt alt. Tant, que ha atrapat Frank van den Broek (Picnic PostNL). En canvi, els altres dos escapats —Felix Engelhardt i Alex Molenaar— han mantingut l’avantatge fins al final del coll de Begues.  El gran grup o pilot ha fet la seva feina i finalment ha agafat  Felix Engelhardt  (Jayco AlUla) i Frank van den Broek  (Picnic PostNL), els dos escapats que encara quedaven. Ha estat pels carrers de Sant Just , a uns 38 quilòmetres de l’arribada a la meta. I tots junts han entrat a Barcelona per la travessera de les Corts.  També et pot interessar Els carrers de Barcelona vibren amb l’etapa inaugural del Tour de França 2026 Redacció Esports 1 minut de lectura El gran grup arriba a Barcelona, Montjuïc Un cop a Barcelona , els ciclistes han pujat i baixat tres vegades al Castell de Montjuïc . En les primeres posicions hi hem vist gairebé sempre el líder de la prova, el danès Jonas Vingegaard, i, ben a prop seu, hi havia l’eslovè Tadej Pogacar, el gran favorit a fer-se amb la ronda francesa. De fet, l’equip de Pogacar, l’UAE, ha estat qui ha marcat el ritme a la muntanya de Montjuïc. I ha estat Isaac del Toro qui s’ha imposat finalment a Barcelona, amb un temps de 3 h 40 min 01 s. Just enganxat al mexicà, ha creuat la meta en segona posició  Tadej Pogacar . El tercer ha estat  Remco Evenepol ,   amb el mateix temps que el primer i el segon.  A la general el ciclista danès  Vingegaard es manté com a líder amb un temps de  4 h 01 min 48 s, amb sis segons d’avantatge respecte a Tadej Pogacar i 15 respecte de Remco Evenepoel.  Classificació de la segona etapa posició ciclista temps 1 Isaac del Toro 03 h 40 min 01 s 2 Tadej Pogacar – 3 Remco Evenepoel – 4 Jones Vingegaard – 5 Mattias Skjelmose a 3 s 6 Tobias Johannessen a 3 s 7 Romain Gregoire a 3 s 8 Lenny Martinez a 3 s 9 Paul Seixas a 3 s 10 Tom Pidcock a 3 s Classificació general posició ciclista temps 1 Jones Vingegaard 04 h 01 min 48 s 2 Tadej Pogacar a 6 s 3 Remco Evenepoel a 15 s 4 Isaac del Toro a 16 s 5 Juan Ayuso a 19 s 6 Paul Seixas a 42 s 7 Romain Gregoire a 44 s 8 Florian Lipowitz a 45 s 9 Lenny Martinez a 53 s 10 Tom Pidcock a 01 min 0 s

articles.body: Afegeix betevé com a font preferida Prats i sembrats s’assequen amb celeritat des de fa dies a les valls pirinenques i en altres zones del nord del país a causa de l’absència dels ruixats de tarda. En algunes zones fa més de 15 dies que no hi plou de forma apreciable , un fet molt poc habitual a l’estiu, una de les estacions plujoses de l’any. #ds4jul Dia ben serè de cap a cap 35,5°C 14,9°C Demà dia similar al d'avui Avui, capvespre des de Puigcerdà pic.twitter.com/atgnabPEZ0 — Meteo Seu d'Urgell (@MeteoSeu) July 4, 2026 Malauradament, si no plou aviat molts arbres caducifolis (roures, freixes, faigs, bedolls, etc.) patiran un fort estrès hídric i acabaran perdent les fulles abans d’hora . La calor intensa , habitual els darrers anys també als Pirineus, acostuma a veure’s atenuada sovint per la presència de xàfecs de tarda, que contribueixen a rebaixar la temperatura a la tarda i a mantenir el verd de la vegetació. També et pot interessar Calor sense aturador a Barcelona: ja és aquí la segona onada de l’estiu a Catalunya Manel Cascante Torrella 4 minuts de lectura Alguns xàfecs a partir de dimarts, de moment locals i poc abundants pel que fa a precipitació Malauradament, fa dies que la presència de les altes pressions i d’un matalàs d’aire càlid i eixut en altura impedeixen que es puguin generar nuvolades de tarda prou potents i precipitacions. Els mapes del temps albiren una setmana de calor molt intensa a les valls pirinenques, amb registres màxims de més de 35 graus al fons de les valls i de més de 20 ºC a 2.500 metres. També indiquen un lleuger repunt de la inestabilitat a partir de dimarts. Dimarts, 7 de juliol, s’espera que entre la tarda i el vespre puguin formar-se nuvolades que acabin descarregant gotellades, o alguna tempesta de calor, al Pirineu Occidental. Dimecres, 8, i dijous, 9 de juliol, les tempestes de tarda podrien ser una mica més extenses i abraçar també punts del Prepirineu. Els models entreveuen encara alguns xàfecs locals de tarda en dies posteriors a causa de la presència d’un petit embossament d’aire fred en altura cap a l’oest de la península Ibèrica. Malgrat això, la incertesa a hores d’ara és elevada i en cap cas es visualitza un episodi general i abundant de precipitacions. El model de previsió per conjunts centreeuropeu (ECMWF) pronostica la possibilitat de xàfecs de tarda a partir de dimarts a Puigcerdà (veure el gràfic inferior). La incertesa, però, és elevada i els registres de precipitació previstos són força minsos en general . L’estiu, una estació tradicionalment plujosa als Pirineus i en sectors del nord-est de Catalunya Segons el Meteocat, les zones amb major nombre de tempestes anuals es troben entre l’oest de la Garrotxa, el Ripollès i l’est del Berguedà i també cap a l’Alta Ribagorça. Mapa amb la distribució de la mitjana anual de dies de tempesta a partir de les dades de la XDDE, període 2006-2023. Font: SMC La precipitació mitjana de l’estiu supera els 200 litres per metre quadrat a molts sectors dels Pirineus i el Prepirineu, així com de la Garrotxa, Osona i el Lluçanès, tal com es pot comprovar a l’Atles climàtic de Catalunya (font: Meteocat). A més, l’estiu és l’estació més plujosa de tot l’any en molts punts del Berguedà, d’Osona, el Ripollès, el Lluçanès o la Cerdanya. També et pot interessar El nou Atles climàtic de Catalunya presenta contrastos de tota mena Oriol Rodríguez Ballester 4 minuts de lectura El canvi climàtic també està afectant el patró de la precipitació a l’estiu als Pirineus El canvi climàtic està afectant de forma destacada els Pirineus en el cas de la temperatura, però també comencen a veure’s alguns patrons en el cas de la precipitació. Segons el darrer Butlletí d’indicadors de canvi climàtic dels Pirineus del 2024 “totes les estacions de l’any mostren una tendència negativa [disminució] de la precipitació als Pirineus, amb valors més elevats, però sense arribar al nivell requerit de significació estadística, a l’estiu i a l’hivern. L’únic valor de tendència significativa s’obté per a la precipitació de l’estiu a la zona d’influència mediterrània, amb una disminució d’un 25 % (un -3,8 % per decenni) en els darrers 65 anys”. També et pot interessar Canvi climàtic als Pirineus: estius un mes més llargs i menys gel Jordi Vallbona 1 minut de lectura En aquesta època de l’any és difícil que plogui a Barcelona o a la costa. Paradoxalment, l ‘estiu és l’estació més plujosa de l’any a bona part dels Pirineus.  L’explicació de la presència de tempestes de tarda als Pirineus, i l’absència a la costa o al prelitoral, rau en el relleu. Només cal una lleugera inestabilitat atmosfèrica perquè puguin generar-se tempestes als Pirineus. La clau són la calor intensa i l’efecte palanca de les muntanyes, les quals afavoreixen el creixement de les nuvolades. Les tempestes descarreguen a l’entorn de la serralada i com a molt assoleixen punts de l’interior o de la serralada Transversal. articles.headline: Un estiu atípic: els Pirineus, de moment, sense tempestes de tarda

articles.body: Afegeix betevé com a font preferida Aquest cap de setmana Barcelona ha tornat a ser l’epicentre de l’esport mundial amb la celebració de la contrarellotge i la disputa de la segona etapa del Tour de França 2026 . Aficionats del ciclisme i veïns s’han aplegat a les dues etapes que han tingut lloc a la ciutat. Un cop finalitzada la prova a Barcelona, l’alcalde, Jaume Collboni, ha expressat la seva satisfacció. “Estem contents, exultants , per l’ èxit de participació . Ha estat el gran esdeveniment des dels Jocs Olímpics del 92 “, ha afirmat. L’alcalde ha agraït la feina dels voluntaris, organitzadors, clubs i aficionats. També ha destacat la capacitat de la ciutat per mostrar la diversitat. “Barcelona ha pogut oferir-ho tot: el litoral, paisatges molt diferents… i Montjuïc ha estat la perla de la segona etapa”, ha afegit Collboni. També et pot interessar Isaac del Toro s’imposa a Barcelona en la segona etapa del Tour Carles Alcoyent Corominas 2 minuts de lectura “Ho hem tornat a fer” L’alcalde ha volgut agrair també la paciència de la ciutadania , que, amb els carrers ocupats, ha sortit al carrer per veure el pas dels ciclistes. De fet, només en la primera etapa inaugural, 120.000 barcelonins es van aplegar al llarg dels 20 quilòmetres del circuit per veure passar els esportistes. “ La ciutat ho ha aconseguit, ho hem tornat a fer “, ha conclòs l’alcalde. “Hi ha molta afició pel ciclisme i volem fomentar-la” , ha assenyalat. Segons l’alcalde, el primer repte ha estat l’organització d’aquest l’esdeveniment esportiu. “El Tour ha sorprès perquè hem innovat molt , vam fer una presentació d’equips inèdita “, ha recordat. I el segon repte, sobretot per als assistents, ha estat la calor , que aquests dies ha estat força intensa. Sobre el futur, l’alcalde ha remarcat que no és el moment de parlar de noves fites. “Hem de pair primer els èxits, però estem preparats”, ha comentat. També et pot interessar Els seguidors més resilients del Tour: “Amb sol, amb gorra i el que calgui!” Marc Bermejo 2 minuts de lectura Dues etapes sense cap incidència a destacar L’alcalde ha remarcat també que les dues etapes de la ciutat s’han desenvolupat sense cap incidència. El  Grand Départ del Tour de França  de dissabte va significar “una primera etapa espectacular pels carrers de  Barcelona “: una contrarellotge per equips de  19,6 quilòmetres  que va sortir del Fòrum i va acabar a Montjuïc, i que  va guanyar Jonas Vingegaard . Foto: Joan Mateu Parra (ACN) Un cop disputada la contrarellotge, els ciclistes van marxar cap a Tarragona , des d’on han sortit en la segona etapa fins a arribar a Barcelona. El gran grup ha arribat a la ciutat a les 16.55 h des de l’Hospitalet de Llobregat. Després ha agafat el carrer de Sants fins a la plaça d’Espanya i ha fet un recorregut per Montjuïc, que ha acabat al passeig Olímpic . Els últims metres han estat molt emocionants i igualats. Finalment ha estat Del Toro qui ha entrat en la primera posició i, just al seu costat, ha creuat la línia d’arribada Tadej Pogacar. El tercer ha estat Jonas Vingegaard, amb el mateix temps. També et pot interessar El Tour posa a prova la mobilitat a la ciutat: les rutes paral·leles per moure’s entre restriccions Marta de San Nicolás 2 minuts de lectura
articles.headline: Collboni, exultant amb el Tour: “Ha estat el gran esdeveniment després dels JJOO del 92”

articles.body: Afegeix betevé com a font preferida Des d’aquest dilluns, 6 de juliol, hi haurà obres d’estiu en dues estacions del suburbà. D’una banda, l’estació de Verdaguer, de l’L4 i l’L5, estarà fora de servei fins al 30 d’agost . De l’altra, el transbordament entre l’L1 i l’L3 a l’estació de Catalunya no reobrirà fins al 6 de setembre i, mentrestant, s’haurà de fer pel carrer. Obres d’accessibilitat a Verdaguer Tot i que la resta del recorregut funciona amb normalitat, els trens de l’L4 no faran parada a Verdaguer. L’objectiu és facilitar el pas pel passadís que connecta amb l’L5, especialment per a persones amb mobilitat reduïda, ja que fins ara havien de pujar i baixar escales . També et pot interessar L’L1 i l’L9/L10 Nord del metro tindran talls parcials durant pràcticament tot l’estiu Jaume Planagumà 2 minuts de lectura Els treballs tenen un pressupost de 8,6 milions d’euros i inclouen la instal·lació de tres ascensors , el recrescut de les andanes i la substitució de les escales ja esmentades per rampes. A més, s’hi afegiran més escales mecàniques i s’eixamplaran les escales fixes existents. Des de l’octubre, estan en marxa els treballs al passadís, que ja estava tancat, i ara han començat la resta d’actuacions. 300 m de transbord pel carrer L’altre passadís que també tanca és el que connecta l’L1 i l’L3 a l’estació de Catalunya. TMB renovarà tots els elements d’aquest punt, farà tasques de revestiment vertical i millorarà les comunicacions . Les obres també preveuen retirar l’amiant que encara hi ha, tot i que no es troba exposat als usuaris. A aquestes tasques s’hi destinaran 1,2 milions d’euros . També et pot interessar Tallat un tram de l’L9/L10 Nord per les obres del macropou de la Sagrera Marta Casals Torres 1 minut de lectura Els usuaris que vulguin anar de l’L1 a l’L3 hauran de sortir i tornar a entrar per l’accés del carrer de Pelai amb la Rambla. Per fer l’intercanvi invers, hauran d’escollir entre l’entrada que hi ha a la cantonada de la plaça de Catalunya a tocar de la ronda de Sant Pere o l’accés nord. El recorregut que s’haurà de fer pel carrer és d’uns 300 metres . Normalment, en un dia laborable, aquest transbord el fan unes 43.000 persones. TMB recorda que caldrà validar de nou el títol, però no serà descomptat cap viatge.
articles.headline: L’estació de Verdaguer i el transbord de Catalunya de metro, tancats durant tot l’estiu

articles.body: Afegeix betevé com a font preferida El restaurant veneçolà Franca , al carrer de Roger de Llúria, 46 , ha tornat a demostrar que la solidaritat i la gastronomia poden ser un bon binomi . Si la setmana passada van aconseguir recaptar 4.200 € i repartir més de 300 arepes , aquest diumenge ho han tornat a fer amb cues encara més llargues . De fet, en aquesta segona tongada de l’arepada solidària han reunit més voluntaris per poder arribar a tothom de manera més àgil. Les llargues cues solidàries davant del Franca També et pot interessar Arepes, “golfeados”, “cachapas” i “raspados” solidaris per recaptar diners per Veneçuela Carmen Cortés Vidal 3 minuts de lectura El plat nacional de Veneçuela L’arepa és l’entrepà més famós de Veneçuela, l’origen del qual es disputen amb Colòmbia. Batalles gastronòmiques a banda, es tracta d’un panet rodó, la massa del qual s’elabora amb farina de blat de moro. Al Franca n’ofereixen les tres versions més populars: la farcida d’alvocat i pollastre, coneguda com a reina pepiada ; la de carn entatxonada i formatge groc, la pelúa , o la de formatge telita , típic veneçolà. Les tres opcions es poden acompanyar de beguda i, òbviament, la millor opció és el papelón , una beguda tradicional veneçolana que es fa amb panela, gel i llimona, i és especialment refrescant ara a l’estiu. La cuina del Franca bull d’activitat per servir les arepes Entre els assistents hem trobat l’Omar , un barceloní assidu del Franca que volia col·laborar amb la comunitat veneçolana. “Aquestes catàstrofes poden afectar qualsevol i sempre està bé poder donar suport als veïns. Ens agrada menjar i quina excusa pot ser millor [per fer-ho] que aprofitar per ser solidaris”. La família de l’Omar s’ha demanat els tres tipus d’arepes i un papelón ben fred. D’esquerra de dreta, dues arepes reina, tequeños, la pelúa amb formatge groc i, al fons, l’arepa telita La Isabella és veneçolana i ella i els seus amics s’han apropat al Franca gràcies al boca-orella de les xarxes. “Han estat uns dies molt durs i ens sentim com si fóssim en un altre planeta, en una espècie de núvol de sentiments . No podem ser allà amb ells, ens han cancel·lat bitllets de vol i a vegades sembla impossible poder arribar on hi ha la família”. Ella i els seus amics aprofiten l’arepada solidària per trobar-se amb més compatriotes . “Ens ha sorprès com els veneçolans de la ciutat ens hem mobilitzat per aquesta acció solidària, deixant de banda per un dia la ideologia i la política. N’estem molt orgullosos!”. 100 % solidària En les darreres setmanes , la comunitat gastronòmica veneçolana de la capital catalana ha unit forces per activar una xarxa de suport benèfic . L’objectiu és clar: recaptar fons per donar suport a les famílies afectades pel terratrèmol a Veneçuela. A través de plats tradicionals com les arepes , els golfeados , les cachapas i els raspados , diversos restaurants i fleques de la ciutat han convertit les seves cuines en un espai d’esperança . Els cartells solidaris del restaurant Franca En el cas del restaurant Franca, un dels seus integrants, en Gianmarco Greci , extreballador del Mugaritz , ens explica així la iniciativa de l’arepada solidària: “Tots estem molt afectats emocionalment de veure el que està passant. Tinc molts amics treballant en associacions a Veneçuela i el consens de la majoria és que ara mateix el que necessiten són diners . Es necessiten per solucionar coses molt directes com comprar medicaments. La necessitat és molt alta, el país trigarà molt de temps a recuperar-se i els cicles mediàtics són curts, així que volem aprofitar ara per recaptar el màxim de diners”. En Gianmarco insisteix que el 100 % del que es recapta va a parar a mans de les associacions humanitàries i aquí, a Barcelona, estan agrupades sota el nom de SOS Veneçuela Barcelona , que en una setmana ha aconseguit gairebé 17.000 € d’ajudes. També et pot interessar Veneçuela viu el terratrèmol més potent en 126 anys: les ones arriben a Barcelona minuts més tard Marcos Amores Sánchez 3 minuts de lectura
articles.headline: Arepada solidària: quan la gastronomia veneçolana salva vides

articles.body: Afegeix betevé com a font preferida L’obra d’ Antoni Gaudí, Joan Miró i Joaquim Gomis dialoguen a la Casa Batlló amb instal·lacions digitals inèdites en una exposició que vol oferir una mirada contemporània a les seves figures. A partir del 8 de juliol, la mostra “Gaudí-Miró-Gomis: Deconstructed” obrirà noves maneres d’acostar-se a les peces originals dels artistes gràcies a la tecnologia. Es tracta d’un projecte de l’estudi Tomorrow Bureau , comissariat per Joana Seguro (Casa Batlló Contemporary) i Ester Ramos (Fundació Joan Miró), en el marc de l’ Any Gaudí . També et pot interessar Neix l’Acadèmia de les Arts Digitals per donar visibilitat a un sector consolidat Víctor Giralt Jonama 1 minut de lectura La proposta reuneix peces original de Miró (escultures i obra gràfica), juntament amb fotografies de Gomis, que va ser clau en la difusió i relectura de l’obra de Gaudí. Les intervencions tracen les relacions que s’estableixen entre els tres creadors, que van compartir un mateix ecosistema cultural i una conversa encreuada al voltant de temàtiques com la natura, la matèria, la vida, l’instint i l’esperit . Però lluny d’aproximacions històriques, el recorregut reflexiona sobre com les eines digitals contemporànies poden activar el patrimoni i obrir noves mirades. Segons Ramos, els tres artistes comparteixen “ una mirada precursora i visionària ”, que l’estudi creatiu ha pogut rellegir.  Miró-Unearthed L’exposició s’articula a partir de dues peces audiovisuals. La primera, Miró-Unearthed , parteix d’una digitalització d’escultures originals de Miró mitjançant la fotogrametria d’alta resolució . Amb tècniques de reconstrucció digital i anàlisi de superfícies, la peça revela detalls, textures i materials invisibles a simple vista. “ Amb la il·luminació i les tècniques científiques acabes veient quelcom fantàstic ”, ha valorat Seguro. La peça, creada per Tomorrow Bureau, situa les obres en diàleg amb els paisatges que van marcar l’imaginari de Miró i Gaudí, des de Montserrat fins al cap de Creus. Gomis – Living archive La segona instal·lació, Gomis – Living archive , pren com a punt de partida les fotografies que Joaquim Gomis va dedicar a l’arquitectura de Gaudí. A través de sistemes d’intel·ligència artificial generativa desenvolupats específicament per al projecte, les imatges originals són reinterpretades i transformades en noves composicions visuals que exploren com la tecnologia contemporània pot ampliar les formes de mirar i comprendre un arxiu històric. També et pot interessar Una fotografia inèdita descobreix com era la làpida original de la tomba de Gaudí Clara Vera Colina 3 minuts de lectura Les instal·lacions s’acompanyen d’una obra sonora creada a partir d’enregistraments de camp realitzats en diferents punts de Catalunya i que construeix una atmosfera immersiva que connecta paisatge, matèria i memòria. La mostra es podrà visitar fins al 10 de gener del 2027 a la segona planta de la Casa Batlló.
articles.headline: La Casa Batlló acull l’exposició “Gaudí-Miró-Gomis: Deconstructed”

articles.body: En ple mercat d’estiu, un dels noms que més sonen al Barça és el de Julián Álvarez . El davanter de l’ Atlètic de Madrid podria ser el substitut de Robert Lewandowski si el club blaugrana aconsegueix arribar a un acord amb el club matalasser. Julián ja ha comunicat públicament que vol deixar l’equip madrileny i, al Barça, els jugadors tenen clar que el rebrien amb els braços oberts. “M’encantaria tenir-lo com a company. A més, jo crec que és el seu somni”, ha dit el blaugrana Gavi en una entrevista a Mundo Deportivo . Podria ser una picada d’ullet a les paraules que va deixar anar Julián Álvarez quan va dir públicament que volia marxar de l’ Atlètic de Madrid. “El millor per a tots és un traspàs, vull fer realitat el meu somni” va dir l’argentí. Gavi también desea el fichaje de Julián Álvarez por el Barça: "Ojalá, es su sueño" 𝐄𝐧𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐯𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐚 𝐌𝐃 a Pablo Gavi Ya completa en YouTube y en la web de Mundo Deportivo @martinezferran @JASIRVENT pic.twitter.com/PBYo3hvHuY — Mundo Deportivo (@mundodeportivo) July 5, 2026 “Soc molt calent al camp i això no agrada a tothom” El migcampista del Barça ha parlat en la concentració de la selecció espanyola al Mundial , abans de disputar els vuitens de final contra Portugal. Més enllà d’opinar sobre la possible arribada de Julián Álvarez al club blaugrana, també ha parlat sobre les crítiques. “Potser molta gent m’està esperant perquè soc un jugador que va fort al xoc i hi ha gent a qui no li agrada aquest tipus de jugadors. Soc un jugador molt calent en el camp, que sempre va a mort amb els seus. El millor de mi és aquesta competitivitat, aquesta intensitat que tinc dins el camp. Mai canviaré, ja sé que sempre hi haurà gent esperant”, ha dit Gavi . També et pot interessar El Barça torna a la regla 1:1 i podrà fitxar amb normalitat en el mercat Carles Molet 2 minuts de lectura També et pot interessar Mundial 2026, el més gran de la història: primers aparellaments de quarts Carles Molet 5 minuts de lectura
articles.headline: La confessió de Gavi sobre Julián Álvarez: “Crec que el seu somni és jugar al Barça”

articles.body: A betevé notícies t’expliquem què està passant a Barcelona amb una mirada àgil, connexions en directe i informació del temps destacada. El rigor, la professionalitat i la proximitat continuen sent els pilars dels informatius de betevé.
